skill.name: claude-d3js-skill
skill.description: Create custom D3.js data visualizations, including charts, network diagrams, maps, scales, color schemes, and interactive SVG patterns for JavaScript applications.
skill.summary: Build interactive D3.js charts and SVG visualizations with reusable patterns and reference guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were largely false positives caused by D3.js terminology, JavaScript template literals, Markdown code blocks, and example color values. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, shell execution, credential access, cryptography misuse, or data exfiltration. A medium risk remains because tooltip examples use D3 .html() with interpolated data, which can cause XSS if copied for untrusted datasets.

medium_findings.title: Unsanitized HTML Tooltip Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill includes tooltip examples that interpolate data values into D3 .html() output. If copied into an application that visualizes untrusted labels or categories, this can render attacker-controlled HTML and create cross-site scripting risk. Use text rendering or sanitize values before inserting HTML.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly builds HTML strings from data fields such as label and category. This is a real XSS risk when source data is not trusted, although the examples do not themselves exfiltrate data or execute external commands.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Credential Alerts Are D3 False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported many shell execution, reconnaissance, weak cryptography, and credential findings. Reviewed examples are JavaScript template literals, Markdown code fences, D3 scale domains and ranges, SVG gradients, color names, node-link terminology, and example object keys. No evidence found of shell execution, credential access, cryptographic code, system reconnaissance, or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed the flagged syntax appears inside D3 visualization examples and documentation. The suspicious labels are explained by chart terminology and JavaScript string interpolation rather than operating system, credential, or crypto operations.

low_findings.title: Documentation References External CDN Script
low_findings.description: The skill shows the official D3 CDN script tag as one setup option. This is a normal documentation pattern, but users should pin trusted versions and follow their project security policy for third-party scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL is clearly the public D3 CDN example, not an exfiltration endpoint. It remains a supply-chain consideration if users copy CDN loading into production pages.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsanitized HTML Tooltip Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes tooltip examples that interpolate data values into D3 .html() output. If copied into an application that visualizes untrusted labels or categories, this can render attacker-controlled HTML and create cross-site scripting risk. Use text rendering or sanitize values before inserting HTML.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly builds HTML strings from data fields such as label and category. This is a real XSS risk when source data is not trusted, although the examples do not themselves exfiltrate data or execute external commands.

content.value_statement: Custom data visualizations are hard to structure when charts need unique layouts, interactions, or SVG control. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical D3.js patterns for charts, maps, networks, scales, colors, and responsive interactions.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of custom D3.js charts with scales, axes, margins, labels, and SVG elements.
Provides examples for bar charts, line charts, scatter plots, heatmaps, treemaps, chord diagrams, and network graphs.
Explains interactive patterns such as tooltips, zooming, panning, brushing, dragging, and animated transitions.
Includes reference material for D3 scale types, color schemes, accessibility, and responsive sizing.
Supplies starter React templates and sample datasets for basic and interactive visualizations.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not a packaged charting library or runtime component.
Generated code still needs testing with real data, target browsers, and application styling.
Large datasets may require canvas, aggregation, or virtualization beyond the included SVG examples.
Tooltip examples should be adjusted to avoid unsafe HTML when data comes from untrusted sources.

use_cases.title: Build Product Analytics Charts
use_cases.description: Create custom dashboards with time series, grouped bars, and responsive SVG interactions for product metrics.

use_cases.title: Prototype Data Journalism Graphics
use_cases.description: Design publication-quality visual stories with careful color choices, labels, annotations, and custom chart layouts.

use_cases.title: Implement App-Specific Visual Tools
use_cases.description: Add network diagrams, maps, brushing, zooming, and hover details to JavaScript or React applications.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Chart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple visualization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the D3.js skill to create a simple bar chart for this dataset. Include scales, axes, labels, and a responsive SVG layout.

prompt_templates.title: Add Interactions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the D3.js skill to add tooltips, hover states, click selection, and zoom behavior to my scatter plot. Avoid unsafe HTML for untrusted data.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Specialized Visualization
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for nonstandard chart types.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the D3.js skill to build a force-directed network diagram from nodes and links. Include drag behavior, color grouping, labels, and performance notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review And Harden D3 Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the D3.js skill to review this visualization code for accessibility, responsiveness, performance, color choices, and XSS-safe tooltip rendering.

output_examples.input: A request for a quarterly revenue bar chart with labels and accessible colors.
output_examples.output: A structured D3 implementation plan with scale choices, axis setup, SVG layout, color guidance, and testing notes.

output_examples.input: A request to make a scatter plot interactive with zoom and tooltips.
output_examples.output: An interaction design that describes hover behavior, selected-point state, zoom limits, tooltip content, and safe text handling.

output_examples.input: A request for a network diagram from nodes and weighted links.
output_examples.output: A force-layout approach with node grouping, link strength, drag behavior, label placement, and performance constraints.

best_practices: Define data shape, chart type, scales, and interaction goals before generating D3 code.
Use semantic colors, sufficient contrast, labels, and accessible SVG titles or descriptions.
Render untrusted tooltip values as text or sanitize them before using HTML output.
anti_patterns: Do not use D3 for simple charts when a standard charting library already fits the requirement.
Do not render thousands of SVG nodes without considering aggregation, canvas, or performance limits.
Do not copy tooltip HTML examples into production without handling untrusted data safely.

faq.question: Does this skill create complete D3.js applications?
faq.answer: It provides patterns, templates, and implementation guidance. The final application still needs integration, testing, and project-specific styling.

faq.question: Can it work with React?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes React-oriented templates and explains when to let D3 manage the DOM or only calculate layout values.

faq.question: Does it support interactive visualizations?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers tooltips, hover states, click selection, zooming, panning, brushing, dragging, and transitions.

faq.question: Can it help with color selection?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for categorical, sequential, diverging, and color-blind-safe D3 color schemes.

faq.question: Is the generated code production ready?
faq.answer: It can be a strong starting point. Review accessibility, responsiveness, performance, data validation, and tooltip safety before production use.

faq.question: Why is the security risk rated medium?
faq.answer: Most scanner alerts are false positives, but tooltip examples interpolate data into HTML. That pattern can create XSS risk with untrusted data.
